observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is a historical canonical snapshot rather than a complete live projection.
M-004 resolves accepted additive records and M-005 validates supplied inventory entries.
Before this slice, no pure discovery-manifest layer explicitly separated missing, duplicate, rejected, and accepted queue-update candidates.
inferred: Repository discovery completeness and individual record validity are different claims and must be represented separately.
proposed: Connector adapters should exhaustively paginate queue-update paths, verify exact file bytes and immutable commits, then pass only complete manifest entries to M-005.
superseded: Treating a successful search response or connector return order as proof of exhaustive queue-update discovery.
unresolved: The available GitHub connector did not expose a single exhaustive directory-listing call for operations/queue-updates during this run.
The pure adapter depends on its caller to verify file bytes and commit identity.
A structurally complete manifest does not prove claims contained inside queue-update records.

acceptance_behavior: requires verified file bytes and immutable 40-character commit identities
requires parseable queue-update content with queue item identity and owner
reports duplicate paths and duplicate blob identities without replacement
reports expected records that were not discovered
emits exact path, commit, and content entries compatible with M-005
retains blob SHA, queue item identity, and owner as provenance metadata
marks the manifest incomplete when any record is missing, rejected, or duplicated

verification.test_result: 6 passed, 0 failed in local Node runtime before committing byte-identical source and tests

privacy_boundary: Public repository paths, commit identifiers, abstract queue metadata, and non-sensitive operational claims only. Raw private chat, health material, credentials, identifying personal data, and unreviewed uploads excluded.
